title: decider end-of-run sweep: migrate status='closed' entries from open-issues.json to closed-issues.json with canonical status normalization; one-time burndown of 11 stranded entries from 2026-05-18 through 2026-05-21
related_findings: FND-02 in monitor/tinker/report-2026-05-25T02-48.json (11 status='closed' entries in open-issues.json with no matching closed-issues.json record)

executive_summary.problem_one_line: 11 issues marked status='closed' in open-issues.json have NO matching entry in closed-issues.json. The decider LLM has been improvising a 'mark as closed in place' shortcut at multiple close sites (M1-BAU-wontfix, already-resolved, stranded-patch, superseded). The canonical migration path is documented (decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md Step 8, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md Step 1) but is bypassed by these improvised sites, and Step 5b in decider-reporting.md only sweeps status='fixed' or 'wontfix' above a >50-entry threshold — neither condition fires for these.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Add an end-of-run normalization sweep to decider that (a) walks open-issues.json for any status='closed' entries, (b) maps each to a canonical close-status (fixed | wontfix) based on its fixed_by field, (c) moves to closed-issues.json, (d) appends a closure-ledger line if one doesn't already exist for that iss_id, (e) writes both files back. Plus a one-time burndown of the 11 stranded entries.
executive_summary.blast_radius_one_line: Low: a 30-line addition to the decider end-of-run section. The sweep is idempotent and self-healing — re-running is safe. Burndown is mechanical (read 11 ids, classify, migrate).

problem_detail.code_location: Multiple. The canonical close path lives in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md Step 1 (sets status='fixed-pending-verification' and moves between files). M1 sweep at monitor/prompts/decider.md line 280-365 flips status to 'assigned-analyst' or 'pending-human' but does NOT close in place. Yet the closed-issues.json log shows 11 entries that were left with status='closed' in open-issues.json (and no matching closed-issues entry). The improvised close sites are LLM-authored ad-hoc inside the decider context, not documented prompts — examples: 'already-resolved-prior-run' (5 entries 2026-05-18T00:20), 'M1-BAU-wontfix' (1 entry 2026-05-21), 'BAU-wontfix-already-fixed' (2 entries 2026-05-21), 'superseded-by-ISS-NNNN' (1 entry 2026-05-21), 'decider-self-apply-stranded-patch' (ISS-2094, blocked because target file is NEVER_PUSH — see FND-01), 'decider-self-apply-generate-html' (ISS-2102).
problem_detail.step_5b_threshold_gap: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-reporting.md line 158-160: 'If open-issues.json has >50 entries with status fixed or wontfix older than 7 days, move them to closed-issues.json'. Two reasons this never fires: (1) status='closed' is not in the filter (only 'fixed' or 'wontfix'); (2) the >50 threshold means smaller batches sit forever. Current open-issues.json has 34 entries — well under 50.

direct_evidence.post_migration_counts: After migration: open-issues.json drops to 23 entries (34 - 11 status=closed). closed-issues.json grows by 11. Tinker's open_issues_total metric reads 23 instead of the inflated 34.

downstream_impact.open_issues_total_inflation: Tinker Mode 1 reports open_issues_total=34 today, but 11 of those (32%) are already-resolved residue. Real open count is 23. Inflated metric crosses PROP-030 thresholds spuriously and produces ghost-old entries (ISS-2094 at 7d+ status='closed' shows up in oldest_open_age_days even though closed).
downstream_impact.decider_workflow_confusion: Decider's M1 sweep filter is 'status === open' (not 'in {open, closed}'), so the 11 stranded entries are correctly skipped by M1 — but a Burndown-mode pass that filters status='closed' could re-process them and produce contradictory ledger entries. The status enum is effectively non-canonical and any future contributor reading open-issues.json will be unsure what status='closed' means.

proposed_fix.summary: Three changes: (1) add an end-of-run normalization sweep to decider; (2) tighten Step 5b in decider-reporting.md to include status='closed' AND lower/remove the >50 threshold; (3) one-time burndown script for the 11 stranded entries.

files_to_modify.find: ## End-of-Run Step A: Analyst Attention Inbox
files_to_modify.explanation: Inserts a new Step A0 before the existing Step A. The sweep mechanizes status normalization at end-of-run so any improvised close site that leaves residue gets caught.

files_to_modify.find:
## Step 5b: Archive Closed Issues

If open-issues.json has >50 entries with status "fixed" or "wontfix" older than 7 days, move them to closed-issues.json (append). Keep only open + recently-closed in active file.
files_to_modify.replace:
## Step 5b: Archive Closed Issues (PROP-056-updated, 2026-05-25)

End-of-Run Step A0 (in decider.md) handles the in-run normalization of `status='closed'` residue. Step 5b remains as a periodic compaction step for older fixed/wontfix entries:

If open-issues.json has >50 entries with status "fixed", "wontfix", or "fixed-pending-verification" older than 7 days, move them to closed-issues.json (append). Keep only open + recently-closed in active file.

**The status='closed' enum is no longer permitted in open-issues.json after Step A0 runs** — any residue is a sweep failure and should be loud-failed at run-end (see decider.md Step A0 self-test). Canonical statuses for closed-issues.json entries are: `fixed`, `fixed-pending-verification`, `wontfix`, `superseded`. For open-issues.json entries: `open`, `blocked-on-curmudgeon`, `assigned-analyst`, `pending-human`, `pending`.
files_to_modify.explanation: Documents the status enum split between open-issues.json and closed-issues.json. References the Step A0 sweep so future contributors know where the normalization happens.

files_to_create.purpose: One-time burndown for the 11 currently-stranded entries. Idempotent: re-running on a clean (already-migrated) tree skips everything. Safe to run before or after the Step A0 sweep is deployed (the sweep will pick up new residue going forward; this burndown clears the existing residue).

tradeoffs.what_we_gain: open_issues_total reflects real backlog. No more ghost-aged status='closed' entries inflating PROP-030 thresholds. Status enum is well-defined per file (open-issues.json vs closed-issues.json). Closure ledger stays in sync via the Step A0 ledger-append.
tradeoffs.what_we_might_lose: 30-line addition to decider.md end-of-run section. Step A0 runs every decider run (~6×/day) but is a near-no-op when there's no status='closed' residue. Token cost negligible. The mapping heuristic ('wontfix' if fixed_by contains 'wontfix|superseded|already-resolved|fuse-sync-gap', else 'fixed') is best-effort — a future close site might not match and would land as 'fixed' when 'wontfix' would be more accurate. Mitigation: the original fixed_by field is preserved on the migrated entry, so audit can always disambiguate.

edge_cases.ID_already_in_closed_issues_json: Step A0 sweep checks `closedIds.has(iss.id)` and skips the migration (drops the open-issues residue as a dup). This handles the case where someone partially migrated and forgot to remove from open-issues.json.
edge_cases.ledger_already_has_entry: Sweep checks `existingLedger.has(iss.id)` and skips the ledger append. Safe for retro-run after PROP-055 backfill (which also writes ledger lines).
edge_cases.interaction_with_PROP_055: PROP-055's ledger-append happens at canonical close-time. PROP-056's Step A0 sweep ledger-append happens at end-of-run for residue. Both check existingLedger before writing, so they don't double-append. Order: PROP-055 runs first (during patch close-issues step), PROP-056 Step A0 runs in end-of-run section. If PROP-055 is deployed but PROP-056 isn't, residue accumulates and tinker re-flags. If PROP-056 is deployed but PROP-055 isn't, ledger still gets entries via the Step A0 sweep (slightly less granular mechanism enum, but still complete).
edge_cases.self_test_failure: If Step A0 fails (e.g., write permission), self-test fires and decider aborts before commit. open-issues.json may have been mutated in memory but not written. Next run re-attempts. Safe.
edge_cases.race_with_workspace_sync: open-issues.json and closed-issues.json are written by the decider in its clone, committed, pushed. workspace-sync may have a concurrent FUSE copy. Standard PROP-051 multi-writer protections (pull --rebase, integrity gate, non-concurrent scheduling) cover this case.

verification_pattern: grep -q 'Step A0: status' monitor/prompts/decider.md && test -f build-scripts/burndown-status-closed-residue-2026-05.js && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
verification_pattern_post_burndown: node -e "const fs=require('fs');const oi=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/decisions/open-issues.json','utf8'));const stranded=oi.issues.filter(i=>i.status==='closed');console.log(stranded.length===0?'FIXED':'STILL_BROKEN: '+stranded.length+' status=closed entries remain in open-issues.json: '+stranded.map(i=>i.id).join(','));"
why_human_needed: Three questions for the operator: (a) Is the status-mapping heuristic ('wontfix' if fixed_by contains wontfix|superseded|already-resolved|fuse-sync-gap, else 'fixed') the right default? PROP defaults to this — feedback welcome. (b) Should ISS-2094 (the FND-01 false-closure) be excluded from the burndown? Tinker recommends YES — see operator_action_required block below. (c) Should Step 5b's >50 threshold be lowered? PROP keeps it at 50 since post-migration the size is well under that; arguments for lowering to e.g. 20 are welcome.

operator_action_required.primary: Apply files_to_modify + files_to_create from a clean clone.
operator_action_required.ISS_2094_caveat: ISS-2094 is the FND-01 false-closure (decider self-apply path failed because target is NEVER_PUSH, but ISS was marked closed). Recommend EITHER: (a) operator re-opens ISS-2094 in open-issues.json with status='pending-human', deletes from the TARGET_IDS list before running the burndown — then operator applies the stranded patch via own-clone path and tombstones the sentinel, OR (b) operator leaves ISS-2094 in the TARGET_IDS list, lets it migrate as 'fixed' (factually wrong but matches the closure metadata), and SEPARATELY re-opens it via a fresh ISS. Tinker recommends option (a) for honesty. Edit the script's TARGET_IDS to remove 'ISS-2094' before running --apply if you prefer (a).
operator_action_required.verification_after_apply: Run the verification_pattern_post_burndown snippet. Expected output: 'FIXED'.
operator_action_required.tinker_followup: On next tinker run, FND-02 will mark FIXED via the verification_pattern_post_burndown. FND-03 (PROP-055) closure-ledger gap will also be partially-resolved by the burndown's ledger appends. PROP-030 net_velocity_7d will return to truth.

estimated_impact.tokens: ~30 lines added to decider.md end-of-run section. Step A0 self-test runs every decider invocation but is a near-no-op (single filter + zero-length branch).
estimated_impact.metric_correctness: open_issues_total drops from 34 to 23 today. PROP-030 oldest_open_age_days drops from 44.3 days (ghost-aged ISS-2094) to ~14d (real oldest). PROP-030 threshold scorers see real-state numbers.
estimated_impact.risk_reduction: Eliminates a confusing non-canonical state from the open-issues.json schema. Future contributors reading open-issues.json see a clean status enum.

rollout_plan: 1. Operator (or future tinker Mode 4 with self-apply empowerment) edits monitor/prompts/decider.md and monitor/prompts/reference/decider-reporting.md per files_to_modify.
3. **DECIDE on ISS-2094 caveat** (see operator_action_required.ISS_2094_caveat). Edit TARGET_IDS if choosing option (a).
4. From clean clone: `node build-scripts/burndown-status-closed-residue-2026-05.js --dry-run`. Verify expected migration list.
6. Commit + push from clean clone with message: 'PROP-056 apply: status=closed normalization sweep + burndown 11 stranded entries'.
7. Verify next decider run's Step A0 reports 'no status=closed residue'. Verify next tinker run reports open_issues_total ≈ 23.
8. Update this PROP's status to 'implemented'. Archive the one-time burndown script (or keep as reference; safe to re-run).
scope_authority: Tinker authors PROP. Operator applies (consistent with PROP-051/PROP-054/PROP-055 pattern).
